name: How do HOA and architectural review rules in Hillsborough and San Bruno affect contractor planning?

mainEntity.name: What should I ask a general contractor about HOA and architectural review board rules in Hillsborough and parts of San Bruno before committing to a remodel or addition?
mainEntity.text: What should I ask a general contractor about HOA and architectural review board rules in Hillsborough and parts of San Bruno before committing to a remodel or addition?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Hillsborough and certain San Bruno neighborhoods with HOAs or architectural review boards, the biggest hidden risk is assuming the city permit is the only approval needed. When interviewing general contractors, ask whether they have worked under your specific HOA or town design guidelines before. A prepared contractor should be able to describe typical aesthetic restrictions on rooflines, exterior materials, fencing, and landscaping. Clarify who is responsible for preparing and submitting the HOA or architectural review package—drawings, finish schedules, neighbor notifications, and any required story poles. Ask whether they attend board meetings or expect you to handle presentations and design negotiations. You should also ask how much time these reviews typically add before permits can be pulled and construction can start. Discuss how HOA rules might constrain practical decisions such as window placement, solar panels, exterior color palettes, and privacy features. Ask the contractor how they build contingency into the schedule and budget for board‑requested revisions. Finally, ensure that the contractor’s written proposal explicitly lists HOA or board coordination as a task, with clear limits on included meetings or resubmittals. This helps avoid surprise fees or delays and ensures your project’s approvals strategy is as deliberate as its construction plan.
acceptedAnswer.description: For Hillsborough and HOA‑regulated areas of San Bruno, ask your general contractor how they incorporate CC&R, design guidelines, and architectural review timelines into the project plan. Clarify who prepares submittal packages, attends board meetings, and revises drawings, and how these requirements affect schedule, costs, and material choices.
